title: rhinanthus serotinus subsp. halophilus
intro: Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. halophilus, also known as halophilous downy ravel or sea plantain, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. halophilus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herb in treating various health conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
